found_specs.title: OpenList README (project overview, feature list, storage drivers)
OpenList Security Policy (supported versions, vuln reporting, user best practices)
OpenList Contributing Guide (build, dev workflow, driver template)
cmd/ - CLI subcommands (admin, server, user, storage, cancel2FA, crypt, lang, restart, kill)
cmd/flags - global CLI flags (data dir, config path)
server/ - HTTP, WebDAV, FTP, SFTP, S3 server entry points
server/handles/ - HTTP handler package (auth, fs, admin, sharing, sso, webauthn, ldap, task, etc.)
server/handles/sshkey.go - SSH key management for SFTP
server/middlewares/ - auth, signature, rate-limit, HTTPS, sharing, search middlewares
server/webdav/webdav.go - WebDAV protocol handler (METHODs, locking, properties)
internal/ - core packages (fs, op, authn, sign, sharing, conf, db, driver)
internal/op/meta.go - per-path meta (password, hide, read/write users) lookup
internal/op/sshkey.go - SSH key store for SFTP server
internal/sign/ - URL signature (HMAC) for download/preview links
internal/conf/config.go - main config struct (DB, scheme, JWT, log, CORS, S3, FTP, SFTP)
internal/errs/ - typed errors (PermissionDenied, ObjectNotFound, MetaNotFound, etc.)
drivers/ - all storage driver implementations (60+ drivers)
drivers/base/ - shared driver HTTP client (rest helpers)
drivers/local/ - Local filesystem driver (path traversal sink)
drivers/quark_uc_tv/ - Quark/UC TV driver (path may vary)
pkg/sign - HMAC signature helpers (used by internal/sign)
pkg/ - reusable library packages (aria2, qbittorrent, cookie, gowebdav, sign, mq, pool)
GHSA-qmj2-8r24-xxcq - Path Traversal in file copy and remove handlers
GHSA-2hw3-h8qx-hqqp - XSS Attack in built-in Markdown Viewer
Installation - Docker (CLI + Compose, env vars OPENLIST_ADMIN_PASSWORD, UMASK)
Installation - Run from source (Go 1.24+, frontend build)
Installation - Reverse proxy (Nginx, Apache, Caddy, aaPanel; Host & X-Forwarded-Host)
Driver - S3 (AWS, R2, B2, OSS, COS, TOS, BOS, Kodo, OBS, etc.)
Driver - AutoIndex (XPath scraping of nginx/apache index pages)
Advanced - FTP/SFTP server, FTPS, passive ports
Advanced - S3-compatible server (expose OpenList as S3 bucket)
Advanced - Meta (per-path password, read/write users, hide regex, write-content bypass)
Advanced - Offline download (Aria2, qBittorrent, Transmission, SimpleHttp, 115/PikPak/Thunder)
Configuration - config.json reference (force, site_url, cdn, jwt_secret, token_expires_in, DB, scheme, log, tasks, CORS, S3, FTP/SFTP)
Configuration - Site (title, announcement, indexing/mounting toggles, robots.txt)
Configuration - Preview (text/audio/video/image extensions, iframe/external preview, OnlyOffice, readme autorender, script filter)
Configuration - Global (hide-files regex, package download, expire time, sign-all, WebAuthn, hooks, OCR)
Configuration - Other (Aria2, qBittorrent, 115/PikPak/Thunder offline-download, fixed Token)
Configuration - Side panel (upload modal, local settings)
FAQ - How-to (passwords, guest upload, reverse proxy subdir, port change, forgot password)
FAQ - Why (compatibility, preview, upload, cloud-storage caveats)
OpenList API live OpenAPI explorer (Chinese mirror)
Ecosystem - OpenList Mobile (Flutter file server app)
Ecosystem - OpenList Proxy (Cloudflare Workers traffic proxy)
OpenList-Docs source repository (canonical Markdown of every doc page)
OpenList-Docs source - all driver Markdown files (canonical driver-doc index)
OpenList-Docs source - all advanced Markdown files
OpenList-Docs source - all configuration Markdown files
OpenList-Docs source - all FAQ Markdown files

notes: doc.openlist.team 302-redirects to doc.oplist.org/ (same content). doc.oplist.org.cn mirrors the same site for Chinese users (same /guide /configuration /faq /api /ecosystem paths). Only the canonical English version is enumerated to avoid duplicates per the out-of-scope rule.

The canonical source-of-truth for documentation is the OpenList-Docs Markdown repo (OpenListTeam/OpenList-Docs). The /pages/guide/drivers and /pages/guide/advanced directory listings gave the authoritative complete page set; not every page has been individually fetched, but every URL listed maps 1:1 to a real .md file in that repo.

API documentation is rendered live at fox.oplist.org / fox.oplist.org.cn (OpenAPI explorer). The static doc page at /api/apidocs only links out; the full endpoint catalog is most reliably extracted from the Go source under server/handles/ and server/router.go.

Three published security advisories define the historical vulnerability classes for this target: GHSA-qmj2-8r24-xxcq (path traversal in fs copy/remove), GHSA-wf93-3ghh-h389 (insecure TLS default), GHSA-2hw3-h8qx-hqqp (XSS in built-in Markdown viewer).
Out-of-scope items (OpenListApp/OpenListApp forks, paid proprietary 'OpenList'-named software, third-party storage provider SDKs/APIs, blog/release-notes, translated duplicates) were excluded.

Some advanced topics referenced in the docs but lacking a dedicated page (e.g. /guide/advanced/sharing, /permission, /ldap, /hooks, /proxy, /ssh-key) returned 404; the underlying mechanism is described inline in the source under server/handles/sharing.go, server/middlewares/auth.go, server/handles/ldap_login.go, internal/op/hook.go, server/handles/sshkey.go etc., and those source paths are included instead.
